title: Local DB dev/template refresh + worktree DB auto-heal correctness

tasks.title: Write DB lifecycle + refresh runbook docs
tasks.description:
Update docs/guides/worktree-dev-environments.md with:
- Canonical one-command DB refresh procedure: `pnpm db:fresh-dev` on hospeda_dev (DESTRUCTIVE — wipes and re-seeds local dev DB) followed by `bash ~/.claude/skills/worktree/scripts/wt-db.sh build-template` to rebuild hospeda_template from the freshly migrated hospeda_dev.
- Warning that db:fresh-dev runs `docker compose down -v` which destroys the local dev DB — document what is lost and when to use it.
- Step-by-step migration cadence: when a new spec ships schema changes (new tables, extras), the refresh must be re-run before cutting new worktrees.
- Reference to AC-2 (drift detection) so readers know stale worktree DBs are now auto-healed.

Key files: docs/guides/worktree-dev-environments.md (existing, extend the DB section).
No code changes. No tests. Acceptance: the guide contains a 'DB refresh' section covering the two-command flow, the destructive warning, and the cadence recommendation.

tasks.title: Add schema-drift detection + auto-heal to wt-db.sh ensure-ready path
tasks.description:
Extend ~/.claude/skills/worktree/scripts/wt-db.sh (outside the repo, parametrized by .claude/project.config.json) to detect a stale DB clone and heal it.

The EXISTING auto-heal path handles DB with NO schema (empty clone) by running db:migrate + db:apply-extras. The GAP is a clone that HAS a schema but is STALE — it was cloned from an old template that predates newer migrations.

Implementation:
- Add a schema-version check in the ensure-ready step: after cloning the template, compare a known sentinel (e.g., check for the existence of a table introduced in the most recent migration, such as `experiences` or a custom schema-version marker) against the cloned DB.
- If the sentinel is absent (stale clone detected): log a clear message ('stale DB detected, healing...'), run `pnpm db:migrate` + `pnpm db:apply-extras` + `pnpm db:seed` (or the project's configured seed command) against the worktree DB.
- If the sentinel is present (schema is current): proceed as today (no-op).
- The sentinel table/marker should be configurable via .claude/project.config.json (e.g., a `schemaVersionSentinel` key listing the table to probe) so it can be updated when new migrations ship without touching the skill script.

Key files: ~/.claude/skills/worktree/scripts/wt-db.sh (primary change), .claude/project.config.json (add schemaVersionSentinel or equivalent config key).

Acceptance (AC-1 + AC-2): a worktree created from a stale template is healed at wt:up time (logged), schema matches current TS schema after heal; a worktree created from a current template is NOT redundantly re-migrated.

subtasks.title: Add sentinel-based stale-clone detection to wt-db.sh
Add auto-heal path (db:push + apply-extras, no reseed) on stale detection
Manual smoke: stale template clone heals cleanly; current clone is a no-op (verified)

tasks.title: Verify commerce seed data is present after wt:up (gastronomy + experience listings)
tasks.description:
Confirm that after running `wt:up` (which heals if stale per T-002), the worktree DB contains commerce example data: at least one gastronomy listing, one experience listing, and a COMMERCE_OWNER user — all required for SPEC-249/SPEC-252 workflows.

If the seed step in T-002's auto-heal path does NOT produce commerce data (because db:seed does not include commerce fixtures in its current form), document the gap and provide the manual command sequence. If it does produce commerce data, document which seed users/listings to expect.

Key files:
- packages/seed/CLAUDE.md (update test-user matrix if commerce owner seed users are confirmed)
- docs/guides/worktree-dev-environments.md (add commerce-data verification step to the wt:up smoke checklist)

Note: seed CONTENT fixes for SPEC-252 (loggable experience owner, profile_completed) are OUT OF SCOPE here (live in SPEC-252). This task only verifies the existing seed produces usable commerce data and documents what exists.

Acceptance: after wt:up on a fresh worktree, `psql -c 'SELECT count(*) FROM gastronomies'` returns > 0 and `psql -c 'SELECT count(*) FROM experiences'` returns > 0; result documented in worktree-dev-environments.md.

subtasks.title: Smoke wt:up fresh worktree and count gastronomy/experience rows (6 gastronomies, 5 experiences, 3 commerce owners — no heal)
Confirm commerce seed (CommerceOwners/Gastronomies/Experiences) loads; seed/CLAUDE.md already covers the test-user matrix, counts documented in worktree-dev-environments.md
